signals.headline: South Korea's March 2026 annual CPI inflation rose to 2
signals.raw: South Korea's March 2026 annual CPI inflation rose to 2.2% YoY (from 2.0% in February), undershooting market forecasts of 2.3-2.4% due to government fuel caps offsetting oil price surges.**[1][2][3] | NO | HIGH confidence
matched_markets: Will South Korea’s 2026 Annual Inflation be between 2.7% and 2.9%?

signals.headline: Key details:** This data, released around April 2, 2026 by Statistics Korea, showed monthly CPI up 0
signals.raw: Key details:** This data, released around April 2, 2026 by Statistics Korea, showed monthly CPI up 0.3% (below 0.6% expected), with core inflation dipping to 2.2% YoY; oil drove rises in transportation (5% YoY), but food fell to 0.5% and measures like fuel caps limited impact.[1][2][3] Bank of Korea held rates at 2.5% (sixth meeting), adopting wait-and-see amid Middle East tensions and oil >$100/b
